expectations.evidence: Naive Claude does not ask the user about model selection before proceeding. Without the skill's AskUserQuestion step (Step 1.5), it would either ignore a 'run with Haiku agents' instruction entirely or interpret it ambiguously — there is no structured model-selection dialogue.

Naive Claude does not spawn Task sub-agents at all, so there is no mechanism to pass a model parameter to them. Even if the user explicitly says 'use Haiku', naive Claude would not translate that into model: "haiku" in parallel Task agent configs.

expectations.text: Reports which model each persona used
expectations.evidence: With no agents launched and no model selection step, there is nothing to report. Naive Claude would produce a single inline response with no model provenance information.

notes: Without the skill, model selection is entirely absent. Naive Claude does not solicit a model choice, does not spawn model-parameterised agents, and produces no output that records which model analysed which persona. All three expectations fail.
